% Generated support snippets from ScytaleDroid cutoff-window and static audit outputs.
% These snippets are optional support material; keep manuscript claims aligned with the 14-day cutoff-window model.

% Auto-generated descriptive results block.
The final cutoff dataset contained 15 of 15 Android consumer applications with build-backed evidence bundles and 0 true evidence holes. The selected runtime evidence included 123 PCAP-backed valid runs: 44 strict idle runs, 23 quiescent foreground runs, and 56 interactive runs. QFG captures are reported separately from strict idle because they represent no-touch foreground observations in which app-driven network activity continued.

At the app level, the descriptive Spearman association between high/medium static finding count and selected PCAP count was -0.2498; the association between high/medium static finding count and observed domain count was 0.0717. These app-level associations are descriptive only and should not be interpreted as causal or predictive evidence. No composite static scoring value is reported because no reviewed formula is approved for this publication profile.

% Baseline-relative runtime bridge from the published Paper #2 method family.

The dynamic behavior exports support a baseline-relative runtime analysis: non-root PCAP-backed sessions are separated into baseline and interactive observations, then summarized using traffic-shape and TLS-fingerprint features. The current manuscript reports these as baseline-relative traffic-shape evidence, because the selected publication bundle is based on aggregate baseline-vs-interactive feature summaries rather than the original published RDI tables.
